- Native Swift and Kotlin are better, but can also take time

Foundation Models in iOS can read the contents of apps running in the simulator.

Paul Hudson just discovered this by accident while rebuilding Copilot Recall on macOS.

The privacy-first on-device AI story Apple's been selling? This is the API surface that makes it real.

Teaching Claude to QA a Capacitor mobile app:

Android: 90 minutes setup via CDP
iOS: 6+ hours of edge cases

• Can't type "@" via AppleScript
• Can't dismiss native dialogs
• Had to write directly to TCC.db

Now runs 25-screen sweeps daily and auto-files bug reports.
